untranslatable_reason: Diamond pattern detection (equal highs/lows, relative width comparison, pattern shape analysis) requires specialized pattern recognition beyond point indicators. Specific requirements: (1) identification of diamond formation with equal top highs and equal bottom lows, (2) measurement of pattern width relative to median width, (3) distinction between 'short & narrow' vs 'tall & narrow' vs 'short & wide' patterns based on formation geometry. These geometric properties cannot be expressed using only OHLC price bars and the allowed technical indicators.
translation_notes: Pattern-based signals like EEDB require multi-bar geometric analysis and comparative width metrics that exceed the scope of point-in-time indicator values. A production implementation would need a dedicated diamond pattern detector module that identifies the formation, measures its dimensions, and classifies it by aspect ratio before applying entry/exit logic.
